calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories To Pltf & For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-07-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant J. Gerlach's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is moot as further responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of said responses. The parties are ordered to meet and confer. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to respond in a timely fashion and served non-responsive answers. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $440 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

RULING; NO ARGUMENT and the court adopted its tentative ruling.GRANT, NO OPPOSITION FILED.Prevailing party to prepare order. (302/REQ/ju)BELDON YEE #2647

Ntc Of Mo To Compel Ans To Post Judgt Disco
Off Calendar Apr-20-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to post judgment interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to provide complete responses without objection within 21 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $336.30 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
